title: [Task Sprint] QA Testing Report — GoThru Maps Frontend

issues.display_title: Issue #47: Fix Blank Map on View Detail Nearest Panorra
issues.main_description_html:
🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #47: Fix Blank Map on View Detail Nearest Panorra

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/panorra-blank-map-detail.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production — https://panorra.com (Find nearby → View detail) + https://maps.panorra.com/thumbnail-v2/<slug>
Result: 🐞 BUG CONFIRMED — present in production

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: one Task-Sprint scenario broken into White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot at the end of each step, and a single continuous recording (address-bar mode so the panorra.com URLs are visible). Reproduces your exact flow: Find nearby posts → hover a thumbnail → View detail.

❌ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box Find nearby posts → hover → View detail opens the post detail (panorama viewer). ✅ Flow works
2 Gray Box Sample the map-thumbnail image endpoint on 10 random nearby posts + a known-good control. ✅ Captured
3 Black Box The post-detail map thumbnail should render. 🐞 FAIL — blank on 10/10 nearby posts
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass (0 5xx)

The panorama detail shows a small map thumbnail (.view-panorama__map-thumbnail, bottom-left). It is blank for posts whose thumbnail hasn't been generated: the <img> has naturalWidth = 0 (broken). Sampled 10/10 random nearby posts (Jakarta) → all blank; a control post renders → it's a per-post bug, not a site-wide outage.

Task Sprint tags: @regression @sanity_test @task_sprint @viewer @#panorra @bug

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/app/thumbnail-v2/[slug]/route.ts

  • 🐞 Root cause: the route fetches ${API_SOURCE_INTERNAL}/panorra/thumbnail/<slug> and, if the response is ok (HTTP 200), returns the body as Content-Type: image/png without checking it's actually an image (route.ts:15‑26). For posts whose thumbnail isn't generated, that upstream returns HTTP 200 with JSON (the post data, {"statusCode":200,"data":{…}}), so the route serves JSON labelled image/png. With x-content-type-options: nosniff, the browser refuses to decode it → the <img> is broken → blank map.
  • The S3 + map-placeholder.png fallbacks (and the queuePanorraGenerateThumbnail call) only run on a 404 (route.ts:29), so a "200-but-not-an-image" response never falls back and never queues generation — it just renders blank.
  • Recommended fix: verify the upstream response is really an image (check content-type starts with image/, or sniff PNG/JPEG magic bytes) before forwarding; otherwise treat it like the 404 path (queue generation → S3 → placeholder). That guarantees a non-blank map for every post.

Test code — panorra-blank-map-detail.spec.ts / panorra-nearby-detail.page.ts: reproduces Find nearby → View detail (trusted mouse click on the hover overlay), reads the detail map thumbnail (naturalWidth), and samples thumbnail-v2/<slug> for 10 real nearby posts (collected from the live panorra tiles) + a control, asserting the endpoint returns a non-image (JSON) for the blank ones and a real PNG for the control.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Endpoint Status Body Result
maps.panorra.com/thumbnail-v2/<slug> (10 blank posts) 200 JSON (mislabelled image/png) 🐞 blank map
maps.panorra.com/thumbnail-v2/ef7bfdd1af7-… (control) 200 PNG 255×255 ✅ renders
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

⚠ Note: the endpoint returns HTTP 200 (not a 5xx) — the defect is the body/content-type mismatch, so the 5xx check is clean while the map is still blank.

🐞 Bug notes

  • Post-detail map thumbnail is blank for posts without a generated thumbnail (10/10 sampled nearby posts; e.g. t0f331adc9e-…, i582d9cacd3-…). A post with a thumbnail (ef7bfdd1af7-…) renders fine.
  • Root cause + fix above (validate upstream content-type in thumbnail-v2/route.ts).
  • Severity: medium — affects the map on the "nearest panorra" post detail for many posts; the panorama itself loads fine.
  • Test runs against production → keep this issue open until the route fix is deployed; re-run (Step 3 flipped to expect 0 blanks) to verify.

🎥 Evidence note

The recording (address-bar mode) shows panorra.com → Find nearby → hover → View detail → the post detail; the map thumbnail is highlighted with a red box labelled "map thumbnail (BLANK)" so the empty area is unmistakable. Screenshots capture the flow and the blank map.

🎥 Test Evidence

47-blank-map-view-detail.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #49: Create Map Embed Access for User 30842
issues.main_description_html:
Tour: https://tourmkr.com/F16O2OXaZl
Reference: https://maps.gothru.co/embed/access/4x9yd9Cyfa45JIyM

🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #49: Create Map Embed Access for User 30842

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/user-tour-map.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production https://maps.gothru.co
Result: 🐞 BUG — embed access for user 30842 is missing (not yet created)

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: one Task-Sprint scenario broken into White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot at the end of each step, and a single continuous recording (address-bar mode so the routes are visible).

❌ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box User 30842's tour renders on the moderation route (/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW). ✅ Pass — tour renders
2 Gray Box The rendered tour belongs to user 30842 and matches tourmkr F16O2OXaZl. ✅ Pass
3 Black Box An embed access (/embed/access/<id>) exists for user 30842. 🐞 FAIL — 404 Page not found
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass (0 5xx)

The tour is on the map and renders fine on the moderation route maps.gothru.co/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW (blue tour line + pano markers, ~8–10k indigo px), and the tile data confirms it's user 30842's tour (tour_key 6O2OXaZl, the core of tourmkr F16O2OXaZl). But the embed ACCESS that #49 asks to create does not exist: maps.gothru.co/embed/access/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW returns "404 Page not found" (no map). The reference embed access …/embed/access/4x9yd9Cyfa45JIyM renders, proving the embed-access mechanism itself works — it just hasn't been created for user 30842.

Task Sprint tags: @regression @sanity_test @task_sprint @viewer @#moderation_map @bug

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/app/embed/access/[accessId]/page.tsx + src/components/main/maps/container/main/MapContainer.tsx

  • The embed-access route renders a user-scoped map ({userId}_source.geom) for a valid access id. gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW is a moderation/stats id (works at /{id}), not an embed-access id — so /embed/access/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW resolves to the 404 Page not found route.
  • No code defect in the embed-access mechanism (the reference id renders). The gap is data/config: an embed access for user 30842 has not been created.
  • Recommended action (dev): create the Map Embed Access for user 30842 (an /embed/access/<id> like the reference) pointing at their tour F16O2OXaZl, then this can be verified + closed.

Test code — user-tour-map.spec.ts / user-tour-map.page.ts: renders the moderation route and decodes the moderation_source.geom tiles to confirm the tour identity (user 30842 / F16O2OXaZl), then asserts the embed-access route 404s while the reference embed access renders.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Route HTTP Content
/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW (moderation route) 200 ✅ renders the tour
/embed/access/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW 200 🐞 client-rendered "404 Page not found" (no map)
/embed/access/4x9yd9Cyfa45JIyM (reference) 200 ✅ renders
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

⚠ Note: the embed-access route returns HTTP 200 but renders a client-side "404 Page not found" — so the 5xx check is clean while the embed access is still missing.

🐞 Bug notes

  • No embed access exists for user 30842 — /embed/access/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW → "404 Page not found". The deliverable of #49 ("Create Map Embed Access") is not yet done.
  • The tour itself is on the map and works via the moderation route; the embed-access mechanism works (reference renders). Only the access for user 30842 needs to be created.
  • Test runs against production → keep this issue open until the embed access is created; then re-run (Step 3 flipped to expect the map to render) to verify.

🎥 Evidence note

The recording (address-bar mode) shows: the tour rendering on the moderation route, then /embed/access/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW showing "404 Page not found", then the reference embed access rendering. Screenshots capture each.

🎥 Test Evidence

49-embed-access-missing.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #91: Update Event Message for Hashtag and Mention on Description Detail Marker
issues.main_description_html:
🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #91: Update Event Message for Hashtag and Mention on Description Detail Marker

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/panorra-hashtag-mention.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production https://maps.panorra.com (panorra marker popup · CustomPopup)
Result: ✅ PASSED

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot per step, one continuous recording. Reuses PanorraPopupPage (#31).

✅ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box The post's marker popup opens with its description. ✅ Pass
2 Gray Box The description renders #hashtag and @mention as clickable links. ✅ Pass
3 Black Box Clicking them emits the event messages (MAP_PANORRA_HASTAG / MAP_PANORRA_USER_MENTION). ✅ Pass
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass

Verified on post dc2920ba9a5-2024020929052214 (Subang, Indonesia): the popup description renders #subang and @deux as clickable links; clicking #subang emits MAP_PANORRA_HASTAG { tag: "subang" } and clicking @deux emits MAP_PANORRA_USER_MENTION { username: "deux" }. (Embedded context forced via a stub window.ReactNativeWebView; standalone the same handlers window.open panorra.com.)

Task Sprint tags: @regression @unit_test @task_sprint @viewer @#panorra

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/components/main/maps/ui/CustomPopup.tsx

  • formatDescription converts #[tag]() → <a onclick="handleHashtagClick('tag')">#tag</a> and @[user](id) → <a onclick="handleMentionClick('user', id)">@user</a> (blue, underlined-on-hover). In embedded context (isEmbeddedContext() — iframe or window.ReactNativeWebView) the handlers sendPostMessage('MAP_PANORRA_HASTAG', { tag }) / ('MAP_PANORRA_USER_MENTION', { username }); standalone they window.open panorra.com. ✅ Confirmed at runtime (both messages emitted with the correct tag/username).
  • No defects found.

Test code — panorra-hashtag-mention.spec.ts / panorra-hashtag.page.ts: opens the post's marker popup (centred on the post's exact tile coords), asserts the description has a clickable #hashtag + @mention, clicks each, and captures the emitted MAP_PANORRA_* event messages.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Endpoint Status
maps.panorra.com/data/panorra_thumbnail_source.geom/{z}/{x}/{y} + openmaptiles/fonts/sprite 200
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

🐞 Bug notes

  • None — hashtags and mentions in the marker description are clickable and emit the correct event messages (MAP_PANORRA_HASTAG / MAP_PANORRA_USER_MENTION).

🎥 Evidence note

The recording shows the marker popup with the clickable #subang / @deux, and clicking each emitting the event message (captured via the embedded-context bridge). Screenshots capture each step.

🎥 Test Evidence

91-hashtag-mention-event.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #11: Fix Bug Event Click Between Embed and Non Embed
issues.main_description_html:
🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #11: Fix Bug Event Click Between Embed and Non Embed

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/embed-nonembed-click.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production https://maps.gothru.co
Result: ✅ PASSED

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot per step, one continuous recording (address-bar mode). Reuses BlueLineClickPage (#79).

✅ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box The non-embed tour route renders the blue line. ✅ Pass
2 Gray Box Non-embed line click opens the pano in a new tab (tourmkr). ✅ Pass
3 Black Box Embed line click does NOT open a new tab (handled in-app). ✅ Pass
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass

The click event correctly differs by mode: on the non-embed route (/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW) clicking the tour line opens the pano in a new tab (tourmkr.com/F16O2OXaZl/<pano>p); on the embed route (/embed/access/4x9yd9Cyfa45JIyM) 4 line clicks opened 0 new tabs — the embed handles the click in-app (socket pano:move) instead of navigating away, keeping the embedding parent in control.

Task Sprint tags: @regression @unit_test @task_sprint @viewer @#moderation_map

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/components/main/maps/source/moderations/ModerationMapEvents.tsx (handleLinesClick)

  • if (!maps.isEmbed) return openTourLines(tourKey, panoId) → non-embed opens a new tab (window.open(tourmkr…)). In embed mode (isEmbed=true, set by /embed/access/*) the handler instead emits socket 'pano:move' (and posts to the parent) — no new tab. ✅ Confirmed at runtime (non-embed opens a tab; embed opens none across 4 clicks).
  • No defects found — the embed/non-embed click behavior is correctly separated.

Test code — embed-nonembed-click.spec.ts / blue-line-click.page.ts: clicks several points along the tour line and captures popup events — asserts the non-embed route opens a tourmkr tab and the embed route opens none.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Route Status
/gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW (non-embed) + tour/openmaptiles tiles 200
/embed/access/4x9yd9Cyfa45JIyM (embed) + tiles 200
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

🐞 Bug notes

  • None — non-embed click opens the pano in a new tab; embed click stays in-app (no new tab). The event-click difference is correct.

🎥 Evidence note

The recording shows a non-embed line click opening a tourmkr tab, then embed line clicks that open no new tab. Screenshots capture the non-embed line, the new-tab open, and the embed no-new-tab state.

🎥 Test Evidence

11-embed-nonembed-click.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #84: Load Photographers Base on Map Location Center
issues.main_description_html:
🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #84: Load Photographers Base on Map Location Center

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/photographer-by-center.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production https://maps.gothru.co (/embed/photographer)
Result: ✅ PASSED

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot per step, one continuous recording (with an on-screen HUD).

✅ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box The photographer embed map opens. ✅ Pass
2 Gray Box Moving the map detects the centre's country (MAP_CENTER_COUNTRY). ✅ Pass
3 Black Box Loading a European country returns its photographers and centres the map there. ✅ Pass
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass

Verified the load-by-centre loop: panning the map emits MAP_CENTER_COUNTRY with the centre's resolved country (e.g. Mali mid-Africa), and loading a European country — Spain (continent 3, country 207) via MAP_LOAD_LOCATION_MARKER returns Spain's photographer markers and flies the map to centre on Spain (post-load MAP_CENTER_COUNTRY = Spain). So photographers load for the country the map is centred on.

Task Sprint tags: @regression @unit_test @task_sprint @viewer @#photographer

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — container/photographers/{MapContainer,PostMessage}.tsx + getPhotographersUserMarkers

  • On moveend, MapContainer calls getGeodataCountriesName(center) and posts MAP_CENTER_COUNTRY (continentId/countryId/countryName) — the centre's location. On MAP_LOAD_LOCATION_MARKER, PostMessage sets the location filter and calls getPhotographersUserMarkers({ continent, country, city }), rendering the returned markers and flying to the location. ✅ Confirmed (centre detected; Spain's photographers loaded + map centred there).
  • No defects found.

Test code — photographer-by-center.spec.ts / photographer-map.page.ts: pans the map and reads MAP_CENTER_COUNTRY (nested geo), then loads a European country and verifies the server-action returns photographer markers (coordinates) and the centre resolves to that country.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Endpoint Status Note
server action → geodata/countries/name (centre → country) 200 MAP_CENTER_COUNTRY
server action → photographers/users/markers (by continent/country) 200 returns markers for the location
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

🐞 Bug notes

  • None — photographers load based on the map centre location (centre-country detection + location-scoped marker load).

🎥 Evidence note

The recording shows the map centre resolving to a country as it pans, then loading a European country's (Spain) photographers and centring there, with the HUD narrating each step. Screenshots capture each step.

🎥 Test Evidence

84-photographers-by-center.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #82: Integrate API All Top Photographer
issues.main_description_html:
🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #82: Integrate API All Top Photographer

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/photographer-top.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production https://maps.gothru.co (/embed/photographer?loadtop=true)
Result: ✅ PASSED

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot per step, one continuous recording (with an on-screen HUD).

✅ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box The photographer embed opens in top mode (loadtop=true). ✅ Pass
2 Gray Box The "all top photographer" API returns the full set and markers render. ✅ Pass
3 Black Box All top photographers are loaded and rendered on the map. ✅ Pass
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass

Verified: ?loadtop=true calls getPhotographersUser and returns 167 top photographers (full set), which render as markers on the map (≈25 in view at the world zoom; the rest cluster off-view). The integration returns and renders all top photographers.

Task Sprint tags: @regression @unit_test @task_sprint @viewer @#photographer

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/app/embed/photographer/page.tsx + src/data/layer/photographers.ts

  • On loadtop=true the page calls getPhotographersUser({ page: 1, size: 1000 }) (a 'use server' action) and maps the returned photographers to markers rendered by the photographer source. ✅ Confirmed at runtime (167 returned, markers rendered).
  • No defects found.

Test code — photographer-top.spec.ts / photographer-map.page.ts: opens the loadtop embed and verifies top photographers loaded via the server-action response (distinct photographer slugs) and/or the rendered-markers message (MAP_PHOTOGRAPHERS_MARKERS_VISIBLE) — robust to RSC-body capture timing.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Endpoint Status Note
POST maps.gothru.co/embed/photographer?loadtop=true (server action → photographers/users) 200 returns 167 top photographers
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

🐞 Bug notes

  • None — the top-photographer API integration returns all top photographers and renders them.

🎥 Evidence note

The recording shows the photographer embed in top mode with the HUD reporting the photographer count; markers render across the map. Screenshots capture each step.

🎥 Test Evidence

82-top-photographers.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #79: Expand the clickable area for the Blue Line on Mobile
issues.main_description_html:
🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #79: Expand the clickable area for the Blue Line on Mobile

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/blue-line-click.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed; desktop + iPhone 13 emulation)
Env: Production https://maps.gothru.co (tour route /gwEEiSgce8QgqSBsrAQW)
Result: ✅ PASSED

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot per step, one continuous recording. Two runs — desktop + a real mobile device emulation (iPhone 13 = "device toolbar": 390×844, DPR 3, touch + mobile UA). Reuses BlueLinePage (#22).

✅ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box The tour blue line renders (desktop + mobile). ✅ Pass
2 Gray Box Clicking on the line opens the pano (baseline). ✅ Pass
3 Black Box Clicking/tapping a bit outside the line (within the hit area) still opens the pano. ✅ Pass
4 Mobile Same off-line touch tap opens the pano on a mobile device. ✅ Pass
5 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass

The reporter wanted to click "a bit outside" the blue line (it was too hard to hit exactly, especially on mobile). Verified: a click/tap 8 px off the visible 5 px stroke still opens the pano (tourmkr.com/F16O2OXaZl/<pano>p) — on desktop (mouse) and on mobile (iPhone 13, real touch tap, line rendered at 10.4k indigo px).

Task Sprint tags: @regression @unit_test @task_sprint @viewer @mobile @#moderation_map

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/components/main/maps/source/moderations/LinesAndPointsByModeration.tsx + ModerationMapEvents.tsx

  • An invisible wide hit layer (moderationIdSourceLayerLinesOutline, line-width: currentLineWidth * 4 ≈ 20 px, line-opacity: 0) sits under the visible 5 px line, and the map click handlers are bound to that layer (map.on('click', 'moderationIdSourceLayerLinesOutline', …)), so a tap within ~10 px of the line centre opens the pano. ✅ Confirmed (8 px off-stroke tap works).
  • Git history matches the fix: the outline hit layer was added, then widened *3 → *4 (15 px → 20 px) — the expansion #79 asked for. The hit area is device-independent (no mobile gating), so it applies on mobile.
  • No defects found — the click area is expanded and an off-line tap registers.

Test code — blue-line-click.spec.ts / blue-line-click.page.ts: finds a point on the blue line and a non-blue point 8 px off the stroke (within the hit area), and asserts both open the pano (popup → tourmkr) — once with a mouse click (desktop) and once with a touchscreen tap (iPhone 13 emulation).

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Endpoint Status
maps.gothru.co/data/{source}/{z}/{x}/{y} (tour tiles) · openmaptiles · fonts · sprite 200
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

No 500/5xx during the flow.

🐞 Bug notes

  • None — the blue-line click area is expanded (≈20 px hit width); tapping a bit outside the line opens the pano on desktop and mobile.

🎥 Evidence note

The recording shows the tour line, clicking on the line (pano opens), then clicking just off the line (pano still opens). Screenshots cover desktop on-line/off-line clicks and the mobile (iPhone 13) line + off-line tap.

🎥 Test Evidence

79-blue-line-click-area.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #52: Implement get Photographer Marker by array of slugs
issues.main_description_html:
🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #52: Implement get Photographer Marker by array of slugs

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/photographer-slugs.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production https://maps.gothru.co (/embed/photographer + postMessage bridge)
Result: ✅ PASSED

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: one Task-Sprint scenario broken into White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot at the end of each step, and a single continuous recording (with an on-screen HUD that prints the slug→marker result).

✅ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box The photographer embed map mounts. ✅ Pass
2 Gray Box Request markers for an array of slugs via MAP_PHOTOGRAPHERS_SLUGS. ✅ Pass
3 Black Box The array returns a marker per valid slug (with coordinates); a non-existent slug returns none (graceful). ✅ Pass
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass

Verified: sending MAP_PHOTOGRAPHERS_SLUGS with an array of 3 slugs (scottie-tuska-vjvh, alin-bugeag-iki6, nick-neal-c24o, type top) returned 3 markers, all 3 slugs matched in the response, each carrying real coordinates (Toronto -79.39, 43.65; UK -3.01, 53.63; Minneapolis -93.27, 44.98) — the pins render on the map. A non-existent slug returns 0 markers (no crash).

Task Sprint tags: @regression @unit_test @task_sprint @viewer @#photographer

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/components/main/maps/container/photographers/PostMessage.tsx + src/data/layer/photographers.ts

  • On MAP_PHOTOGRAPHERS_SLUGS the handler calls getPhotographerMarkers(data) (a 'use server' action) which POSTs to photographers/markers with the message body (the slugs array + type), then setMarkers(markers, type) stores the results and the layer renders them. ✅ Confirmed: the array request returns one marker per valid slug with coordinates.
  • getPhotographerMarkers's param type is still annotated { slug: string } even though callers pass { slugs: string[], type } — a cosmetic type mismatch (no runtime effect, the body is forwarded as-is). Minor cleanup suggestion: type it as { slugs: string[]; type?: 'main' | 'top' }.
  • No functional defects — markers are correctly fetched by an array of slugs.

Test code — photographer-slugs.spec.ts / photographer-slugs.page.ts: posts MAP_PHOTOGRAPHERS_SLUGS and parses the server-action (RSC) response to count the markers returned for the array and confirm coordinates, plus a non-existent-slug resilience check.

📝 Test-data note

The provided main slugs alexander-pro-360 and johndoe-photography returned data:null (they don't resolve on this env), so they couldn't demonstrate the array. The array was verified with the user-provided valid slug scottie-tuska-vjvh plus real top slugs sourced from the live top-photographers list; johndoe-photography is reused as the non-existent-slug resilience case.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Endpoint Status Note
POST maps.gothru.co/embed/photographer (server action → photographers/markers) 200 returns data:[…markers…] for the slug array
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

No 500/5xx responses during the flow. (Any 5xx would be flagged here as a 🐞 bug with status + URL.)

🐞 Bug notes

  • None — markers are correctly returned for an array of slugs, and non-existent slugs are handled gracefully (no marker, no crash). Optional cleanup: fix the getPhotographerMarkers param type annotation.

🎥 Evidence note

The recording shows the photographer map with an on-screen HUD: the array of 3 slugs sent, the 3 markers returned (matched slugs + coordinates), and the markers rendered at their real locations. Screenshots capture each step.

🎥 Test Evidence

52-photographer-marker-by-slugs.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #53: Fix User Detail Popup on Map
issues.main_description_html:
When clicking on a photographer’s user card in the list, the user details on the map do not appear.

🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #53: Fix User Detail Popup on Map

Status: 🗑️ DEPRECATED — not tested (Deprecated = superseded / no longer planned)
Env: n/a — no E2E test performed

Why deprecated

This issue targeted the old GoThru Photographer map UI — clicking a photographer's user card in the list to show that user's detail popup on the map. That photographer marker map has since been redesigned/superseded (see the photographer marker work: #81 Update Marker Map GoThru Photographer — also deprecated — and the current photographer map verified in #82 Integrate API All Top Photographer and #84 Load Photographers Base on Map Location Center). The specific user-detail-popup flow described here no longer applies to the current map, so this ticket is not planned and was not QA-tested.

QA action

  • Labelled Deprecated, assigned ekosy9, closed as not planned.
  • No test summary/evidence produced — there is nothing to verify against the current build (the flow was superseded). Per the Deprecated label, deprecated tickets are not tested.
  • If the user-detail popup is reintroduced on the new photographer map, reopen with the current flow and it will be tested then.

(No Unit Testing/Passed labels — this was superseded, not tested.)

• Make the blue line remains the same size on zooming in and out

🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #48: Update Blue Line Map (same size on zoom)

Suite: qa_testing/e2e/module/maps/blue-line-width.spec.ts (Playwright · Chromium · headed)
Env: Production https://maps.gothru.co (moderation tour map · /ZhFxiX0RLYszj0IKQKN2)
Result: ✅ PASSED

Structure follows qa_testing/qa_template.md: one Task-Sprint scenario broken into White / Gray / Black test.steps, a screenshot at the end of each step, and a single continuous recording. Reuses the #22 blue-line page object.

✅ Test Scenario & Result

Step Type What the test verifies (user scenario) Result
1 White Box The tour map opens and the blue line renders. ✅ Pass
2 Gray Box Measure the blue-line stroke thickness at base / zoomed-in / zoomed-out. ✅ Captured
3 Black Box The blue line stays the same size while zooming in and out. ✅ Pass
4 Response codes No HTTP 5xx during the flow. ✅ Pass

Task: "Make the blue line remains the same size on zooming in and out" (behave like VWalkTour). Verified by measuring the rendered stroke thickness at three zoom levels — it is 5 px at every zoom ([5, 5, 5], drift 0 px). The line keeps a constant on-screen width; it does not grow/shrink with zoom.

Task Sprint tags: @regression @unit_test @task_sprint @viewer @#moderation_map

🔍 Code Review

Feature source — src/components/main/maps/source/moderations/LinesAndPointsByModeration.tsx

  • The colored line layer (moderationIdSourceLayerLines) paints 'line-width': currentLineWidth — a constant (maps.lineStyle?.lineSize ?? DEFAULT_LINE_WIDTH = 5), not a zoom-interpolated expression. MapLibre therefore renders a constant pixel width at every zoom. ✅ Confirmed at runtime (5 px across zoom in/out).
  • Git history matches the fix: 476bcd9 feat: implement dynamic line width adjustment based on map zoom level (the line changing size) → bf65452 refactor: simplify line width handling … (back to a constant width). #48 is implemented by the simplification.
  • No defects found — the blue line is the same size on zoom.

Test code — blue-line-width.spec.ts / blue-line-width.page.ts: loads the tour, then measures the indigo (#6366f1) stroke thickness as the 25th-percentile of contiguous blue run-lengths (a length/orientation-insensitive proxy for stroke width) at base, zoomed-in (−3), and zoomed-out (+6) views, asserting the thickness drift ≤ 2 px.

🌐 APIs Hit & Response Codes

Method Endpoint Status
GET maps.gothru.co/data/{source}/{z}/{x}/{y} (tour MVT tiles) 200
GET maps.gothru.co/data/openmaptiles/{z}/{x}/{y}.pbf · fonts · sprite 200
Check Result
HTTP 5xx (server errors) 0 — none ✅

No 500/5xx responses during the flow. (Any 5xx would be flagged here as a 🐞 bug with status + URL.)

🐞 Bug notes

  • None — the blue line keeps a constant 5 px width across all zoom levels, as requested.

🎥 Evidence note

The recording shows the tour blue line while zooming in and back out; the stroke thickness stays visibly constant. Screenshots capture the line at base / zoomed-in / zoomed-out (all 5 px).

🎥 Test Evidence

48-blue-line-constant-size.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #81: Update Marker Map GoThru Photographer
issues.main_description_html:
• Update Layout https://www.figma.com/design/dYtblSwtmlx5WdPQeURlsP/UI-Design-Gothru-Photographer?node-id=2162-94214&t=Bkp1kmSFmDdlriRg-0
  • Redirect View Photographer Button at Marker Map to Photographer Detail
  • Redirect Portfolio to Open New Tab to GoThru Portfolio

🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #81: Update Marker Map GoThru Photographer

Status: 🗑️ DEPRECATED — not tested (Deprecated = superseded / no longer planned)
Env: n/a — no E2E test performed

Why deprecated

This issue was a photographer marker-map UI revamp (new layout per the Figma, "View Photographer" button → photographer detail, Portfolio → open GoThru Portfolio in a new tab). The photographer marker map has since been superseded by the current implementation, which was verified separately — #82 Integrate API All Top Photographer (✅ Passed) and #84 Load Photographers Base on Map Location Center (✅ Passed). The layout/redirect tasks in this ticket no longer match the shipped map, so it is not planned and was not QA-tested.

QA action

  • Labelled Deprecated, assigned ekosy9, closed as not planned.
  • No test summary/evidence produced — nothing to verify against the current build (superseded). Per the Deprecated label, deprecated tickets are not tested.
  • The current photographer map is covered by the passing tests on #82 and #84.

(Should carry only Enhancement + Deprecated — not Unit Testing/Passed, since it was superseded, not tested.)
